name: 5-Stage AI Build Workflow
description: Primary teaching axis. The build cycle of AI-assisted work, from problem framing to documentation. Custom framing; conceptually compatible with the academic 4D framework but adds Define and Document as bookends.

stages.name: Define
stages.description: Frame the problem. Decide what good looks like before involving any AI. Includes scope, success criteria, constraints, and the human's own understanding of the domain.

stages.name: Delegate
stages.description: Decide what AI does, what stays human. The split is the design choice. Ask: where does AI add value, where does it add risk? Includes choosing the right model, tool, and the boundary of automation.

stages.name: Direct
stages.description: Communicate intent. Includes prompt design, examples, constraints, format specifications, and iterative steering. The act of telling AI what to do, well.

stages.name: Discern
stages.description: Evaluate output. Quality, accuracy, fit, hallucination check, edge cases. Skill-intensive; the place where most AI workflows quietly fail.

stages.name: Document
stages.description: Capture decisions, lessons, attribution. ADRs, postmortems, LEARNINGS files. Closes the loop and makes the next cycle better.
